mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year increase in the CPI reduces real purchasing power and raises inflation expectations, which compresses equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to consistently negative forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year correlation between CPI year-over-year change and S&P 500 20-day returns is not negative or is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth falling below 100,000 signals labor market deterioration that precedes economic contraction, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS monthly change < 100 is followed by positive average S&P 500 60-day returns over any 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized weakening in domestic demand, which reliably predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -2%
failure_condition: When both thresholds are triggered, the proportion of positive S&P 500 20-day returns exceeds 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a federal funds rate tightening cycle (defined by rates above their 12-month moving average), further rate increases compress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 12-month MA), the average 60-day S&P 500 return following such triggers is not negative and significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals supply-side disruption and recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day S&P 500 return after a >1% monthly IP decline is not negative over any 10-year sample, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong year-over-year growth in real GDP boosts corporate earnings expectations and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 5-year correlation between GDPC1 year-over-year growth and S&P 500 60-day returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in industrial production create a classic recessionary signal that strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month change > 0 AND INDPRO month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: In cases where both conditions hold, the average S&P 500 20-day return is not negative and significant at the 95% confidence level over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points triggers immediate risk-off sentiment and cash rebalancing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a >0.2pp monthly UNRATE increase is not negative over any 5-year rolling sample.
